root_text.sn54.5:1.1: “Ānāpānassati, bhikkhave, bhāvitā bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
root_text.sn54.5:1.2: Kathaṁ bhāvitā ca, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati kathaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā?
root_text.sn54.5:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.sn54.5:1.6: Evaṁ bhāvitā kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassati evaṁ bahulīkatā mahapphalā hoti mahānisaṁsā.
root_text.sn54.5:2.1: Evaṁ bhāvitāya kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā.
root_text.sn54.5:2.4: no ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti.
root_text.sn54.5:2.6: no ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti.
root_text.sn54.5:2.7: Atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti …
root_text.sn54.5:2.12: evaṁ bhāvitāya kho, bhikkhave, ānāpānassatiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya ime satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā”ti.

translation_text.sn54.5:1.1: “Mendicants, when mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated it’s very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn54.5:1.2: And how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated to be very fruitful and beneficial?
translation_text.sn54.5:1.3: It’s when a mendicant—gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut—sits down cross-legged, sets their body straight, and establishes mindfulness in their presence.
translation_text.sn54.5:1.4: Just mindful, they breathe in. Mindful, they breathe out. …
translation_text.sn54.5:1.5: They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe in observing letting go.’ They practice like this: ‘I’ll breathe out observing letting go.’
translation_text.sn54.5:1.6: Mindfulness of breathing, when developed and cultivated in this way, is very fruitful and beneficial.
translation_text.sn54.5:2.1: When mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated in this way you can expect seven fruits and benefits.
translation_text.sn54.5:2.3: You attain enlightenment early on in this very life.
translation_text.sn54.5:2.5: If not, you attain enlightenment at the time of death.
translation_text.sn54.5:2.7: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters you’re extinguished in between one life and the next …
translation_text.sn54.5:2.11: you head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm …
translation_text.sn54.5:2.12: When mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated in this way you can expect these seven fruits and benefits.”
